title: Russia Launches Massive Overnight Strike — Dnipro Hardest Hit, 10 Dead
detail: Russia launched one of the largest aerial strikes of 2026, deploying 47 missiles and 619 drones in an overnight attack across Ukraine. Dnipro was hardest hit, with 8 people killed and 49 wounded including 2 children. At least 2 additional civilians were killed in other regions. Ukraine's air defense shot down or suppressed 610 of the 619 drones and most of the missiles. The attack targeted civilian infrastructure, residential apartment blocks, and energy facilities. Ukrainian officials condemned the strikes as deliberate mass terror against civilians.

media.caption: Russia's massive overnight drone and missile strike kills at least 10 across Ukraine

title: Ukraine's Drones Reach Ural Regions — First-Ever Strikes on Sverdlovsk and Chelyabinsk
detail: Ukrainian drones struck the Sverdlovsk and Chelyabinsk regions of Russia for the first time, reaching targets over 1,700 km from Ukrainian-controlled territory in an unprecedented long-range operation. In Sverdlovsk Oblast (home of Russia's industrial heartland including the Yekaterinburg metropolitan area), 6 people were injured. In Belgorod region, 1 woman was killed and 2 men wounded; in Kursk region, 1 additional person was wounded. The strikes represent the deepest penetration of Ukrainian drone attacks into Russian territory since the full-scale invasion began and signal a significant expansion of Ukraine's long-range campaign targeting Russia's industrial and military infrastructure far from the front.

media.caption: First Ukrainian drone strikes reach Russia's Ural regions — Sverdlovsk and Chelyabinsk

title: Zelensky Offers to Meet Putin in Azerbaijan, Discusses Security Guarantees with US
detail: President Volodymyr Zelensky, during an official visit to Baku, offered to meet with Vladimir Putin in Azerbaijan — accepting any neutral country as a venue except Russia or Belarus. Zelensky stated that Ukraine was actively discussing the next negotiation round with the United States, with security guarantees as the central topic. He had previously offered Istanbul, the Middle East, and Europe as venues. The peace diplomacy intensified amid US pressure from the Trump administration, which has sought to broker an end to the war. Ukraine insists any ceasefire must include binding security guarantees to prevent future Russian aggression.

media.caption: Zelensky offers to meet Putin in Azerbaijan during Baku visit
